name: Parvalbumin-positive basket cell
morphology_summary: Perisomatic-targeting interneuron forming dense plexus of inhibitory terminals on soma of pyramidal cells; soma in SP/SO
ephys_summary: Fast-spiking (80% of PV+ INs), capable of sustained firing >200 Hz, bursts up to 150 Hz during ripples; short-duration APs with large rapid AHPs
subtypes_noted: Four morphological subtypes within PV+ INs: basket, axo-axonic, bistratified, radiatum-targeting (Ekins et al., 2020); 7-14 PV transcriptomic subtypes in CA1 (Tzilivaki et al., 2023)

name: Cholecystokinin-positive basket cell
morphology_summary: Perisomatic-targeting interneuron; CB1R+ axon terminals surround somata and proximal dendrites of pyramidal neurons; 96.9% of CCK+ INs express CB1
ephys_summary: Regular-spiking, low in vivo firing frequency (2-10 Hz); during theta oscillations fire at 8.8 +/- 3.3 Hz on ascending phase; 1.7 +/- 2.0 Hz during ripples; episode-dependent firing
subtypes_noted: CCK cells are a remarkably diverse group extending beyond Sncg transcriptomic class (Tzilivaki et al., 2023; Dudok et al., 2021); activity inversely scales with PV cell activity

name: Axo-axonic (chandelier) cell
morphology_summary: PV+ interneuron specifically targeting axon initial segment of pyramidal cells; cartridge-like axonal bouton arrays
ephys_summary: Fast-spiking; controls spike initiation of principal neurons
subtypes_noted: One of three canonical PV+ interneuron subtypes alongside basket and bistratified cells (Ekins et al., 2020; Dannenberg et al., 2017)

name: Bistratified cell
morphology_summary: Dendritic-targeting interneuron with axonal arbor in stratum oriens and stratum radiatum; innervates dendritic domains of pyramidal cells
ephys_summary: Regular-spiking; mediates feedforward inhibition of CA1 pyramidal cells together with basket cells
subtypes_noted: Sst::Tac1 intersectional genetics targets bistratified cells that overwhelmingly target fast-spiking interneurons (Chamberland et al., 2024)

name: Oriens-Lacunosum Moleculare (O-LM) cell
morphology_summary: Soma and dendrites in stratum oriens; axon projects to stratum lacunosum-moleculare; dendrite-targeting interneuron
ephys_summary: Regulates dendritic Na+ and Ca2+ spikes and synaptic plasticity by innervating distal dendritic domains of pyramidal neurons
subtypes_noted: One of two main SOM+ types in CA1 (with hippocampo-septal cells; Takacs et al., 2024); Ndnf::Nkx2-1 intersection selectively targets OLM cells (Chamberland et al., 2024); at least four Sst-IN subfamilies identified

name: Hippocampo-septal (HS) cell
morphology_summary: Long-range projecting SOM+ interneuron with soma in stratum oriens; projects to medial septum
subtypes_noted: One of two main SOM-containing cell types in CA1 alongside OLM cells (Takacs et al., 2024)

name: Neurogliaform cell (NGC)
morphology_summary: Dense, compact axonal arbor providing volume transmission of GABA; small round soma; short radiating dendrites
ephys_summary: Late-spiking phenotype; provides slow, diffuse inhibition of pyramidal cells
subtypes_noted: nNOS+ NGCs derived from MGE share properties with Ivy cells; nNOS- NGCs arise from CGE (Tricoire et al., 2010); transcriptomic identity defined by Lamp5/Id2 expression (Kim et al., 2025)

name: Ivy cell (IvC)
morphology_summary: Dense local axonal arbor; soma typically in or near stratum pyramidale; NOS-expressing; similar to NGC but different laminar position
ephys_summary: Late-spiking phenotype; slow pyramidal cell inhibition; overlapping electrophysiological properties with nNOS+ NGCs
subtypes_noted: Ivy cells and nNOS+ NGCs may constitute a single subtype despite laminar differences (Tricoire et al., 2010); one of most representative CA1 interneuron types (Bocchio et al., 2024)

name: Interneuron-specific (IS) interneuron
morphology_summary: Selectively contacts other interneurons rather than pyramidal cells; plays disinhibitory role in circuit
ephys_summary: Not extensively characterized in this report
subtypes_noted: Three subtypes: IS-1 (CR+), IS-2 (VIP+), IS-3 (CR+/VIP+) (Tyan et al., 2014; Tzilivaki et al., 2023); majority of VIP subclass are IS cells
